questions.question: What is the primary difference between standard LinkedIn search and Sales Navigator search?

options.text: Sales Nav search is faster.
Standard search is for finding people; Sales Nav search is for finding 'Business Value' using deep intent and activity filters.
Standard search only works on mobile.
Sales Nav search allows you to see people's private messages.

questions.explanation: Sales Navigator provides specialized filters (like headcount growth and past lead activity) that help you identify prospects with specific business needs and budget signals.

questions.question: Why is the 'Changed jobs in the last 90 days' Spotlight filter so valuable for solo founders?

options.text: Because new employees have more time to scroll LinkedIn.
Because people in new roles are in 'Change Mode' and are more likely to implement new solutions and build new vendor relationships.
Because it means they just got a pay raise and can afford your service.
It isn't; it's better to target people who have been in their role for 10+ years.

questions.explanation: New decision-makers often want to make an impact quickly. They are more open to new frameworks and tools than someone who is entrenched in an old system.

questions.question: In Sales Navigator, what is the 'Top-Down' approach to list building?

options.text: Adding every CEO in your city to a list.
Building an 'Account List' of target companies first, then finding the specific 'Leads' (decision-makers) within those companies.
Starting with the most junior employees and working your way up.
Searching for terms at the top of the search bar only.

questions.explanation: Effective prospecting starts at the organization level. If the company is a'Good Fit' for your solution, you then find the right people to talk to.

questions.question: What 'Silent Tracking' capability do 'Smart Links' provide?

options.text: They automatically record the prospect's screen.
They tell you which specific prospect viewed your content, when they viewed it, and for how long.
They send an automated DM to anyone who clicks the link.
They hide the content until the prospect pays a fee.

questions.explanation: Smart Links provide 'Intelligence Without Interaction.' You can see if a prospect is actually reading your proposal or case study before you follow up.

questions.question: When combining Sales Nav with the 'Trust Pincer,' which group of prospects should you prioritize for connection?

options.text: People who have over 10,000 followers.
People who 'Posted on LinkedIn in the last 30 days.'.
People who work at the largest companies in the world.
People who haven't updated their profile in 2 years.

questions.explanation: Active users are the only ones who will see your authority-building content (the public arm of the pincer). If they don't use LinkedIn, your content reach is wasted on them.

questions.question: Think about your 'Ideal Customer Profile' (ICP). What is one 'Spotlight' signal (Job Change, Mention in News, Shared Connection, etc.) that would make you feel 100% confident in reaching out to them today? How would you use that specific signal in your connection request to prove you are a practitioner and not a bot?
promptHints: Focus on an 'Event' that triggers the need for your solution.
Think about the language you would use (e.g., 'Congrats on the new role at [Company]...').
Consider how you can bridge their recent activity to your area of expertise.

title: LinkedIn Sales Navigator for Prospecting - Quiz
